title: American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations.
summary: The American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act aims to establish a framework for the responsible development and use of artificial intelligence in the United States. It likely seeks to promote leadership and consistency in AI regulations across the country.

title: Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Education and Workforce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This policy aims to stop artificial intelligence from charging unfair prices or fixing wages. The act of 2025 seeks to regulate AI in order to prevent these practices.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: This policy aims to prevent and punish the theft of American artificial intelligence models, likely intended as a law or legislation to enhance national security and protect intellectual property. The Act of 2026 likely introduces regulations and penalties for individuals and countries found to be involved in the unauthorized use or theft of US-created AI models.

title: Research and Oversight of AI in Courts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This policy document is likely about establishing laws and guidelines for using Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the US court system, aiming to ensure oversight and accountability. The Research and Oversight of AI in Courts Act of 2026 aims to regulate the use of AI in the judicial system by promoting transparency, fairness, and public trust.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a policy document outlining regulations and guidelines for the use of artificial intelligence within the Small Business Administration. Its purpose likely aims to promote responsible AI adoption within the SBA and guide its application to support small businesses.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers from false or misleading information presented by artificial intelligence. This policy is designed to ensure transparency and honesty in AI interactions to prevent potential harm or deception.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This document outlines the Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026, a proposed law aimed at managing and mitigating the risks associated with the use and development of artificial intelligence. It likely establishes regulations and guidelines to ensure the safe and responsible development and deployment of AI technologies.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to promote the use of artificial intelligence in the financial sector. It seeks to create a regulatory environment that balances innovation with the need for safety and oversight.

title: HBCU AI Research Leadership Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The HBCU (Historically Black Colleges and Universities) AI Research Leadership Act aims to advance artificial intelligence research at HBCUs. It likely supports education and resources for AI research at these institutions.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This policy is aimed at promoting AI literacy and readiness among students in K-12 education in the United States, likely through educational programs and resources. The specifics of the policy are unclear without further information, but it suggests an effort to prepare younger generations for an increasingly technology-driven world.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The "Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act" aims to safeguard consumers from misleading or fake AI-driven products and services. This policy likely establishes guidelines and laws to regulate the use of AI in a way that prevents deception and ensures honesty in marketing and sales.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy that aims to provide guidance or regulation regarding the use of artificial intelligence models.

title: SBA AI Adoption Reporting Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Small Business.
summary: The SBA AI Adoption Reporting Act of 2026 is a policy that aims to track and promote the adoption of artificial intelligence in Small Business Administration activities. The policy likely requires reporting mechanisms to measure AI adoption and its effectiveness within the SBA.

title: CREATE AI Act of 2025
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 29 - 0.
summary: The CREATE AI Act of 2025 is a document outlining a policy focused on artificial intelligence, but its exact content is not available. The title alone suggests that the policy likely aims to create standards, regulations, or laws surrounding the development and use of artificial intelligence.

title: Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025 is a policy that addresses the accountability of algorithms in decision-making processes. It aims to ensure that these algorithms are transparent, fair, and unbiased, while also holding the entities responsible for their use accountable for any potential harm caused.

title: Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2025 aims to protect citizens from potential civil rights violations caused by artificial intelligence systems. The act seeks to establish legislation addressing AI-related rights issues.

title: To prohibit the manufacture and conveyance of certain products for children that incorporate an artificial intelligence chatbot, and for other purposes.
action: Sponsor introductory remarks on measure. (CR H3000-3001)
summary: This policy aims to prohibit the creation and transportation of certain products for children that include artificial intelligence chatbots. Its purpose is also to address other related matters.

title: To codify Executive Order 14319 (relating to preventing woke AI in the Federal Government).
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This policy aims to implement an executive order that restricts the use of "woke" artificial intelligence (AI) in the Federal Government. The goal is to prevent AI systems with values or ideologies at odds with the government's stance.

title: To amend the Public Health Service Act to update quality standards for mammography facilities for the use of AI systems, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This policy document aims to update the standards for mammography facilities that use artificial intelligence systems to ensure better quality healthcare. It plans to make changes to the Public Health Service Act to achieve this goal.

title: American Leadership in AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committees on Energy and Commerce, Agriculture, Oversight and Government Reform, Education and Workforce, the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The American Leadership in AI Act aims to promote and advance the development of artificial intelligence in the United States. It likely sets policies and guidelines to maintain the country's position as a global leader in the field.
